tools.name: Context7
tools.description: Fresh, version-specific library documentation. Stops hallucinated API calls.
tools.userFriendlyDescription: Gives your agent access to up-to-date documentation so it stops guessing at APIs and making up function names.
tools.whoActs: I can add this with one command — want me to?
tools.install: claude mcp add context7 -- npx -y @upstash/context7-mcp@latest

tools.name: Exa Search
tools.description: Semantic web search optimized for AI. Sub-200ms. Best accuracy.
tools.install: claude mcp add exa -- npx -y exa-mcp

tools.name: Firecrawl
tools.description: Web scraping that returns clean, LLM-ready markdown.
tools.install: claude mcp add firecrawl -- npx -y firecrawl-mcp

tools.name: Chrome DevTools MCP
tools.description: Agent can see browser output — screenshots, console, network, DOM.
tools.install: claude mcp add chrome-devtools -- npx chrome-devtools-mcp@latest

tools.name: Playwright MCP
tools.description: Cross-browser testing and automation via accessibility tree.
tools.install: claude mcp add playwright -- npx @playwright/mcp@latest

tools.name: Figma Context MCP (Framelink)
tools.description: Design-to-code from Figma frames. Sends structured layout data to agent.
tools.install: claude mcp add figma -- npx -y figma-developer-mcp --figma-api-key=YOUR_KEY --stdio

tools.name: 21st.dev Magic
tools.description: Generate UI components from natural language. Multiple style variations.
tools.install: claude mcp add 21st -- npx -y @21st-dev/magic@latest API_KEY=YOUR_KEY

tools.name: Supabase MCP
tools.description: Full backend access — tables, queries, auth, storage.
tools.install: Remote OAuth: https://mcp.supabase.com/mcp

tools.name: Sentry MCP
tools.description: Production error data, stack traces, breadcrumbs in agent context.
tools.install: Remote OAuth: https://mcp.sentry.dev/mcp

tools.name: Vercel MCP
tools.description: Manage projects, deployments, and domains from Claude Code.
tools.install: Remote OAuth via Vercel

tools.name: GitHub MCP
tools.description: Issues, PRs, CI workflows from the terminal.
tools.install: claude mcp add github -- npx -y @anthropic/mcp-server-github

tools.name: claude-code-prompt-improver
tools.description: UserPromptSubmit hook that intercepts vague prompts and asks clarifying questions.
tools.userFriendlyDescription: Catches vague instructions before the agent starts working — asks you to be more specific so you get better results.
tools.whoActs: You need to follow the setup guide on GitHub — takes about 2 minutes.
tools.install: See github.com/severity1/claude-code-prompt-improver for hook setup

tools.name: Destructive command blocker
tools.description: PreToolUse hook that blocks rm -rf, git push --force, terraform destroy, DROP TABLE.
tools.userFriendlyDescription: Automatically blocks dangerous commands (delete everything, force push, drop database) before they can execute.
tools.whoActs: I can add this to your settings now — want me to?

tools.name: Auto-build after edit
tools.description: PostToolUse hook that runs build after every code change.
tools.userFriendlyDescription: Automatically builds your project after every code change — catches errors immediately instead of at deploy time.
tools.whoActs: I can add this to your settings now — want me to?

tools.name: Protected files guard
tools.description: PreToolUse hook that blocks edits to .env, credentials, package-lock.

tools.name: /learn skill
tools.description: End-of-session learning capture. Reviews corrections, confirmations, and insights. Saves to memory so the agent remembers next time.
tools.install:
Create ~/.claude/skills/learn/SKILL.md with instructions to:
1. Review session for corrections and confirmations
2. Present learnings to user for approval
3. Save as feedback/project memory files
4. Update MEMORY.md index

tools.name: /ship skill
tools.description: Safe shipping workflow. Builds, tests, commits, and pushes in one flow with quality checks at each step.
tools.install:
Create ~/.claude/skills/ship/SKILL.md with instructions to:
1. Run build and verify success
2. Run tests if they exist
3. Git add relevant files (not .env, not node_modules)
4. Commit with conventional commit message
5. Push to remote

tools.name: /research skill
tools.description: Quality-controlled research in 3 phases: Collect sources → Rate + find counter-evidence → Analyze only from verified data. Prevents presenting unverified claims as findings.
tools.install:
Create ~/.claude/skills/research/SKILL.md with 3-phase process:
Phase 1: Find sources, save each with evidence rating (A-F)
Phase 2: Rate sources, search for counter-evidence
Phase 3: Analyze ONLY from saved sources, two-source minimum for findings

tools.name: /standup skill
tools.description: Daily standup briefing. Checks all projects, git status, open tasks, and recommends what to work on today.
tools.install:
Create ~/.claude/skills/standup/SKILL.md with instructions to:
1. Check git status across all projects
2. List open/in-progress tasks
3. Recommend top priorities based on goals
4. Flag blocked items

tools.name: Scheduled Tasks
tools.description: Run prompts on a cron schedule. Daily briefings, weekly syncs, hourly health checks — without a server.
tools.userFriendlyDescription: Lets your agent do things on a schedule — every morning, every Friday, once an hour — without you reminding it.
tools.whoActs: You install this yourself in Claude Code (skill marketplace or config). Quick.
tools.install: Add the scheduled-tasks MCP server: claude mcp add scheduled-tasks

tools.name: Claude in Chrome
tools.description: Browser automation MCP — navigate, click, fill forms, read pages, take screenshots. Real Chrome, not headless.
tools.userFriendlyDescription: Lets your agent open websites for you — fill a form, check a dashboard, grab a screenshot — without you copy-pasting.
tools.whoActs: You install the Chrome extension + MCP server. Takes 3 minutes.
tools.install: Install the Claude in Chrome extension + follow the connection prompt

tools.name: GitHub MCP Server
tools.description: Official GitHub MCP server. Full repo/issue/PR/workflow access via natural language.
tools.userFriendlyDescription: Lets your agent work with your GitHub repos — open issues, review PRs, trigger workflows — without copy-pasting.
tools.whoActs: I can add this with one command — want me to?
tools.install: claude mcp add github -- npx -y @github/github-mcp-server

tools.name: Microsoft MCP Servers
tools.description: Microsoft's official MCP catalog — Azure, Fabric, M365 (Calendar/Mail/Teams/Word), Azure DevOps, Sentinel, SQL.
tools.userFriendlyDescription: Microsoft's entire platform — Azure, Office 365, Teams, SQL — accessible to your agent via MCP. Pick the ones you use.
tools.whoActs: You need to follow the setup guide — each server installs separately, takes about 5 minutes per server.
tools.install: https://github.com/microsoft/mcp — see individual server READMEs

tools.name: AWS MCP Servers
tools.description: AWS official MCP suite — 40+ servers: IaC, EKS, DynamoDB, PostgreSQL, Bedrock, SageMaker, S3, IAM.
tools.userFriendlyDescription: 40+ AWS services exposed as MCP servers. Your agent can query DynamoDB, deploy to EKS, read CloudWatch logs directly.
tools.whoActs: You need to follow the setup guide — each server installs separately, takes about 5 minutes per server.
tools.install: https://github.com/awslabs/mcp — see individual server READMEs

tools.name: IBM MCP Servers
tools.description: IBM's official MCP catalog — IBM Cloud, DB2, watsonx.data, Guardium, QRadar, Instana, Carbon.
tools.userFriendlyDescription: IBM enterprise stack via MCP — useful if you work with IBM Cloud, DB2, or watsonx.
tools.whoActs: You need to follow the setup guide — each server installs separately.
tools.install: https://github.com/IBM/mcp — see individual server READMEs
